paper.title_en: The charge density fluctuations and the Shrinking Fermi Liquid scenario for strange metallicity in cuprates
paper.abstract_en: We interpret the strange metal (SM) properties of slightly overdoped cuprates in terms of the recently proposed Shrinking Fermi Liquid theory. This is based on the pervading presence in the cuprate phase diagram of charge density fluctuations (CDF), which have been identified and characterized in RIXS. These fluctuations are abundant and have a low energy due to the proximity of the charge density wave quantum critical point hidden under the superconducting dome of cuprates, but have a short range and non-critical character with a finite energy M/γ~10 meV as measured above Tc. Here M~ ξ^-2 is determined by the short correlation length ξ, while γencodes the Landau damping ruling the lifetime of the charge fluctuations. Besides these low energy CDF, cuprates also display phonons and a broad continuum of particle-hole excitations, mostly due to spin paramagnons arising from their strongly correlated character. With these experimentally characterized ingredients we show that above Tc the SM properties in transport are well described in terms of fermionic Landau quasiparticles scattering with CDF and phonons. The optical properties can instead be interpreted by the combined effect of low energy CDF determining the temperature dependence, and of the paramagnon continuum determining a linear in frequency scattering rate. Remarkably, the combined effect of these simple ingredients also induces ω/T scaling properties for frequencies larger than M/γ. When superconductivity is suppressed by strong magnetic fields the SM properties extend down to a few Kelvin. By assuming that the CDF dissipation parameter γgrows logarithmically by lowering T, we account for all anomalous transport and thermodynamic properties of cuprates (specific heat, Seebeck, heat transport, resistivity, and magnetoresistance) thereby providing a consistent scenario for the SM phase of cuprates.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文深入解释了铜氧化物奇异金属相的输运和热力学性质，基于电荷密度涨落和收缩费米液体理论，与强关联电子系统和全息对偶相关。
decision.title_zh: 铜氧化物中奇异金属性的电荷密度涨落与收缩费米液体情景
decision.abstract_zh: 我们根据最近提出的收缩费米液体理论来解释轻微过掺杂铜氧化物的奇异金属性质。该理论基于铜氧化物相图中普遍存在的电荷密度涨落，这些涨落已在RIXS中被识别和表征。这些涨落丰富且能量低，是由于隐藏在铜氧化物超导穹顶下的电荷密度波量子临界点的邻近性，但具有短程和非临界特性，有限能量M/γ~10 meV（在Tc以上测量）。这里M~ ξ^-2由短关联长度ξ决定，而γ编码了决定电荷涨落寿命的朗道阻尼。除了这些低能CDF，铜氧化物还表现出声子和宽泛的粒子-空穴激发连续谱，主要源于它们强关联特性产生的自旋顺磁子。利用这些实验表征的成分，我们展示了在Tc以上，输运中的奇异金属性质可以用费米子朗道准粒子与CDF和声子散射很好地描述。而光学性质则可以通过低能CDF决定温度依赖性以及顺磁子连续谱决定线性频率散射率的联合效应来解释。值得注意的是，这些简单成分的联合效应也导致了频率大于M/γ时的ω/T标度性质。当超导被强磁场抑制时，奇异金属性质延伸到几开尔文。通过假设CDF耗散参数γ随温度降低而对数增长，我们解释了铜氧化物所有异常的输运和热力学性质（比热、塞贝克系数、热输运、电阻率和磁阻），从而为铜氧化物的奇异金属相提供了一个一致的情景。
